checks.finding: Every AC-NNN cited in specs/epic-136-phase3/tasks.md (Requirements fields and Done-When bullets) resolves to a real entry in acceptance-tests.md. Direct set comparison of `grep -o 'AC-[0-9]\{3\}'` over both files shows an identical set AC-001..AC-023 on both sides — no dangling ID in either direction.

tasks.md `### Blockers` fields: T-001 line 363 'None', T-002 line 568 'None', T-003 line 797 'T-001, T-002' (bare comma-separated T-NNN list, no range notation, no prose), T-004 line 1083 'None'. precheck-result.json round-2 records blockers_format_valid: true, confirming the mechanical check independently. blockers_format_valid=true.

requirements.md defines REQ-001..REQ-006 (Goals section). Every one is covered by a task's Requirements field: T-001 (tasks.md:212-213) REQ-001/REQ-005/REQ-006; T-002 (:424-425) REQ-002/REQ-005/REQ-006; T-003 (:617-619) REQ-004/REQ-005/REQ-006; T-004 (:853-855) REQ-003/REQ-005/REQ-006. All 6 requirement IDs are present across the 4 tasks.

All 23 AC-NNN rows in acceptance-tests.md (AC-001..AC-023) are traceable to a task: AC-001..007->T-001 Done-When (tasks.md:320-337), AC-008..011->T-002 (:525-538), AC-012..015->T-004 (:1027-1040), AC-016..018->T-003 (:746-758), AC-020->T-003 (:759-761), AC-019/021/022/023 are each independently referenced per-task (each task's own Done-When 'Shared legs' bullet). Note: acceptance-tests.md's Status column (line 26-29) and its header note (lines 7-11) still label AC-012..015/TEST-012..015 'Blocked' against the pre-unblock ADR-0010 snapshot, while tasks.md's re-check section (tasks.md:19-53) explicitly re-verifies ADR-0010 is now Accepted and treats T-004 as a normal (non-Blocked) task covering those same ACs directly in its Done-When. Coverage itself is unambiguous (T-004 cites AC-012..015 explicitly), so this does not fail AC-COVERAGE; the residual acceptance-tests.md Status-column staleness is the same class of intentionally-frozen-column situation tasks.md itself documents for the Test Type column (tasks.md:196-206), not an uncovered AC.

All 4 tasks carry a non-empty Requirements field referencing real REQ-NNN IDs (T-001 tasks.md:212, T-002:424, T-003:617, T-004:853). No task lacks a requirement anchor; no INFRA/HOUSEKEEPING pseudo-requirement is used.

No task title contains 'test'/'spec'/'verify', and no task's scope is exclusively test-file changes (each task's Commit B also touches CHANGELOG.md/doc surfaces per the Global Constraints two-commit convention, tasks.md:86-98), so the narrow per-task test-task definition does not apply to any of T-001..T-004. Applying the round's broader instruction (every TEST-NNN in acceptance-tests.md must be owned by a task): TEST-001..007->T-001, TEST-008..011->T-002, TEST-012..015->T-004, TEST-016..018/020->T-003, TEST-019/021/022/023 owned by each task's own scoped leg (T-001/T-002/T-003/T-004). All TEST-001..023 are owned; none is left unreferenced.

Round 2 of attempt 1 (post round-1 remediation). All 4 tasks carry `Approval: Draft` (T-001 tasks.md:162, T-002:374, T-003:579, T-004:814) and `Status: Planned` (T-001:164, T-002:376, T-003:581, T-004:816). Draft is not Approved and Planned is a valid post-round-1 Status value, satisfying both the round-1 initial-state rule and the subsequent-round rule (Approval != Approved, Status in {Planned, In Progress, Blocked}).

T-001: Risk: medium (tasks.md:166) / Required Workflow: acceptance-first (:190) — matches risk-gate-matrix medium->acceptance-first. T-002: Risk: medium (:378) / acceptance-first (:407) — matches. T-003: Risk: high (:583) / tdd (:611) — matches high/critical->tdd. T-004: Risk: high (:818) / tdd (:847) — matches. precheck-result.json round-2 records workflow_match_precheck: PASS, corroborating no mismatch.

AC-019/AC-021/AC-022/AC-023 are referenced by multiple tasks (T-001, T-002, and/or T-004, T-003), but each occurrence is explicitly scoped to that task's OWN files/issue — traceability.md's Task Mapping table states this verbatim for each task ('TEST-019/TEST-021/TEST-022/TEST-023 legs scoped to this task's files and issue #123/#124/#125/#126', traceability.md:28-31), and tasks.md's own Done-When bullets repeat 'this task's OWN entry citing #NNN' (e.g. tasks.md:341, 542, 763, 1044). This is an explicit per-task scope differentiation, not two tasks silently claiming the same undifferentiated AC as their primary deliverable. AC-020 is claimed only by T-003 (T-004's own leg is explicitly deferred, tasks.md:853-855 and Out of Scope :1069-1077). No unqualified duplicate primary-target claim found.

Round-1 remediation verified across all three artifacts: tasks.md T-003 `Depends On: T-001, T-002` (line 621) and `### Blockers` value `T-001, T-002` (line 797) now agree; both referenced IDs (T-001, T-002) exist as real task headings in tasks.md (## T-001 line 158, ## T-002 line 370) and in dependency-graph.json's `nodes` array `["T-001","T-002","T-003","T-004"]`; the same file's `edges` array now contains `{"from":"T-003","to":"T-001"}` and `{"from":"T-003","to":"T-002"}` (dependency-graph.json:7), matching tasks.md's Blockers field exactly. T-001/T-002/T-004 all declare `None`/`none` Blockers and correctly have no outgoing edges. The round-1 FAIL (empty edges array despite a stated dependency) is resolved.

BLOCKERS-FORMAT is PASS, so this check runs. dependency-graph.json edges: T-003->T-001, T-003->T-002. Neither T-001 nor T-002 has any outgoing edge, so no path can revisit T-003 or any other node. No cycle exists.

T-001/T-002/T-004 titles and scopes each address one coherent Stream's deliverable; the 'and' in each Commit-A/Commit-B split is the mandatory implementation+documentation housekeeping pattern the Global Constraints establish uniformly (tasks.md:86-91, 'two-commit landing plan'), matching the allowed housekeeping exception. T-003's title 'Mark the deterministic CI lane and stage the shared test.yml batch (Streams A/B/D)' connects two aspects of producing ONE staged artifact (specs/epic-136-phase3/human-copy/.github/workflows/test.yml) in a single combined batch — design.md/Global Constraints and the Protected Files section (tasks.md:54-107) explicitly mandate combining these into one staged candidate specifically to avoid 'two sequential human-copy rounds against the same protected file' (tasks.md:104-105). This is a single deliverable with two described facets, required by design to be inseparable, not two distinct feature concerns bolted together.

grep for forbidden phrases ('ensure', 'consider', 'works correctly', 'verify is correct') across tasks.md returned zero matches. Every Done-When bullet across all 4 tasks names a concrete, inspectable artifact or result: named TEST-NNN outcomes tied to AC-NNN (e.g. T-001 tasks.md:320-337), a grep-based self-check outcome (AC-019/021, e.g. :338-344), a named CHANGELOG.md entry (AC-022), an explicit-answer doc-surface record (AC-023), and an implementation-report statement plus an independent quality-gate/review verdict (e.g. :345-351, :767-777, :1048-1057). Each non-documentation task (all 4) includes at minimum a named TEST-NNN/quality-gate-verdict evidence item satisfying the concrete-verification-artifact requirement.

All 4 task IDs (T-001..T-004) appear in traceability.md's Task Mapping table (lines 28-31) and Deliverables table (lines 65-68); all requirement IDs in traceability.md (REQ-001..REQ-006) exist in requirements.md's Goals section — no dangling reference either direction. Full-profile layer-spec anchors verified: security-spec.md#trust-boundaries (heading at security-spec.md:17), infra-spec.md#cicd-sequence (infra-spec.md:39), security-spec.md#stride-analysis (security-spec.md:43), ux-spec.md#scope-and-user-journeys (ux-spec.md:25), frontend-spec.md#technology-stack (frontend-spec.md:11) — all resolve to real headings in the hash-verified layer documents; REQ-006's row uses a reasoned 'N/A — cross-layer only:' value (traceability.md line 13) per the allowed alternative. Limitation: traceability.json was not in this round's allowed-input manifest (only traceability.md was authorized), so its rows could not be independently verified against tasks.md in this pass; this check is scoped to traceability.md, which is fully consistent.
